titleStmt.title: Letter by Edward O'Farrell to R.J. Nugent, 29 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Edward O'Farrell(c.1857-1926) to R.J. Nugent. O'Farrell writes in response to a police report on the illegal importation of glass from Belgium by a Mr. H. Wigoder & Co. in Feb. 1916. The letter is addressed to the Controller, Lancaster House, St. James, London. Sir Edward O'Farrell served as the Assistant Under Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Guest Churchill, Lord Wimborne.

correspAction.persName: Controller, Lancaster House, St. James, London

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
29th February, 1916.
No. 3651. Sir, Referring to your letter of the 19th
instant, No.16473, I am directed by the Lords
Justices to transmit, to be laid before the
Secretary of State, the accompanying copy of a
report which has been received from the Police
respecting the importation by Messrs. Wigoder
& Co., of goods of Belgian origin. The
enclosures to your letter are herewith returned. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, E O'Farrell The Controller, Foreign Trade Department, Lancaster House, St. James, London.S.W.
